blocks.description: Optional rich-text structure. When omitted, the note is stored as a single text block.

text.description: Plain-text internal note. Required, and used as the fallback rendering when blocks are supplied.
text.example: Customer asked for a callback tomorrow.

assignee_user_id.description: ID of the user to assign the conversation to. Pass null to unassign. Omit to leave the assignee unchanged.

inbox_id.description: ID of the inbox to move the conversation to (optional).

activity_data.description: Shape depends on activity_type. assigned: assigned_to, previously_assigned_to, assigned_to_inbox, previously_assigned_to_inbox. unassigned: previously_assigned_to, assigned_to_inbox, previously_assigned_to_inbox. status_change: from, to, reason. priority_change: from, to. comment: text, blocks, mentions.

example.text: Talk to Sarah about the refund

activity_id.description: ID of the activity logged for this change (null if unchanged)

assigned_user_id.description: ID of the assigned user (null if unassigned)

id.description: ID of the created comment activity

activity_id.description: ID of the activity logged for this change (null if unchanged)

email.description: Email address of the user requesting password reset

turnstileToken.description: Cloudflare Turnstile token for bot protection

idToken.description: Google ID token from credential response

detail.example: The requested resource was not found.
The requested resource was not found.

orgName.description: Name of the organization to create

attachment_ids.description: IDs of previously uploaded attachments to include.

body.example: Thanks for reaching out — how can we help?

reply_to_message_id.description: Internal message ID to reply to (WhatsApp only).

channel_id.description: Channel ID to send message through

contact_data.description: Contact data for auto-creation when using "to"

contact_id.description: Contact ID to send message to (mutually exclusive with "to")

to.description: Phone number or email to send to (mutually exclusive with "contact_id")

attachment_id.description: Attachment ID for template header media

status.description: New status for the conversation ("open" or "closed")

detail.example: The requested resource was not found.

info.description:
## Overview

The Interline Public API allows you to programmatically send messages, manage contacts, read conversations, and receive real-time webhook notifications.

This page documents **only** `/v1/...` routes. It is published for customers at [help.interline.chat/api/reference](https://help.interline.chat/api/reference/), rebuilt from this spec on every production deploy. The full authenticated product API (JWT) is internal, and documented separately on the app host at `/api/reference`.

## Authentication

All API requests require an API key, passed as a Bearer token:

```
Authorization: Bearer ilc_live_your_api_key_here
```

You can create and manage API keys in the Interline dashboard under Settings > API Keys.

## Rate Limiting

Authenticated requests are rate limited per endpoint, based on the HTTP method:

- Read requests (`GET`, `HEAD`): 120 requests per minute per API key
- Write requests (everything else): 30 requests per minute per API key

Some endpoints set a stricter limit than these defaults; check the endpoint's documentation for its specific limit.

Authenticated limits are enforced at two levels — per API key, and per organization across all of that organization's API keys combined. The organization ceiling scales with the org's subscription plan, so it is always a multiple of the per-key limit. Requests without a valid API key are additionally limited per IP address before authentication.

Rate limit headers are included in every response, reflecting whichever applicable limit is closest to being reached:

- `X-RateLimit-Limit`: Maximum requests per minute for the binding scope
- `X-RateLimit-Remaining`: Remaining requests in current window
- `X-RateLimit-Reset`: Unix timestamp when the limit resets
- `Retry-After`: Seconds to wait before retrying (sent only on `429` responses)

## Scopes

API keys can be restricted to specific scopes:

| Scope | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `messages:read` | Read message status and content |
| `messages:write` | Send messages |
| `contacts:read` | List and view contacts |
| `contacts:write` | Create and update contacts |
| `conversations:read` | List and view conversations |
| `conversations:write` | Open, close, assign, comment on, and reply to conversations |
| `channels:read` | List available channels |
| `templates:read` | List WhatsApp templates |
| `webhooks:read` | List registered webhooks |
| `webhooks:write` | Create and delete webhooks |
| `users:read` | List and view organization members |

## Errors

All error responses are [RFC 7807](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7807)
Problem Details documents with the media type `application/problem+json`.

```json
{
  "type": "https://api.interline.chat/problems/insufficient-scope",
  "title": "Insufficient scope",
  "status": 403,
  "detail": "This endpoint requires one of the following scopes: contacts:write.",
  "required_scopes": ["contacts:write"],
  "instance": "/v1/contacts"
}
```

The `type` URI is the stable, machine-readable identifier for each error.

| Status | `type` | When |
|--------|--------|------|
| 401 | `.../unauthorized` | Missing or invalid API key |
| 403 | `.../insufficient-scope` | Key lacks a required scope (see `required_scopes`) |
| 404 | `.../not-found` | Resource does not exist |
| 409 | `.../conflict` | Resource conflict (e.g. duplicate) |
| 422 | `.../validation-error` | Request understood but invalid; see `errors` |
| 429 | `.../rate-limit-exceeded` | Rate limit hit; see `Retry-After` |
| 400 | `.../bad-request` | Request was malformed/unreadable |
| 500 | `.../internal-error` | Unexpected server error |

Validation failures (422) include a grouped `errors` array:

```json
{
  "type": "https://api.interline.chat/problems/validation-error",
  "title": "Validation failed",
  "status": 422,
  "detail": "The request body failed validation.",
  "errors": [
    { "field": "contact_id", "messages": ["contact_id must be an integer"] }
  ]
}
```
info.title: Interline API

get.description: Retrieve all active channels for your organization. Returns channel ID, name, type (whatsapp, email, etc.), and status.
Retrieve a paginated list of contacts. Supports search and filtering.

post.description: Create a new contact in your organization. At least one of phone_number, email, or whatsapp_id is required.

delete.description: Permanently delete a contact. This action cannot be undone.

get.description: Retrieve detailed information about a specific contact.

patch.description: Update an existing contact. Only provided fields will be updated.

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of conversations. Supports filtering by status, contact, and channel.

parameters.description: Filter by conversation status (open, closed, snoozed)
parameters.name: status

get.description: Retrieve detailed information about a specific conversation including contact and channel details.
Retrieve a paginated history of assignments, status and priority changes, and internal comments, ordered by most recent first.

parameters.description: Filter by activity type. Repeat the parameter or pass a comma-separated list.
parameters.name: activity_type

post.description: Assign a conversation to a user, or pass a null assignee_user_id to unassign. Optionally move it to a different inbox.

404.description: Conversation or assignee user not found

post.description: Add an internal note to a conversation, optionally with rich-text blocks and user mentions. Comments are not sent to the contact.

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of messages in a conversation, ordered by most recent first.

post.description: Send an outbound message in an existing conversation. A closed conversation is automatically reopened.

put.description: Set a conversation status to "open" or "closed".

post.description: Send a message through a channel. Requires either contact_id or to (phone/email). Requires either body or template_id.

429.description: Too many requests - rate limit exceeded

get.description: Retrieve WhatsApp message templates. Templates must be approved by Meta before they can be used for sending messages.

Retrieve a paginated list of the members of your organization. Removed members are excluded. Use these IDs to resolve `assigned_user_id` on conversations, or to pick an `assignee_user_id` when assigning.

parameters.description: Filter by organization role (admin, manager, agent)
parameters.name: role

parameters.description: Filter by user status (active, inactive, disabled)
parameters.name: status

403.description: Forbidden - API key lacks the users:read scope

get.description: Retrieve a single member of your organization. Returns 404 if the user is not an active member of your organization.

403.description: Forbidden - API key lacks the users:read scope

get.description: Retrieve all registered webhooks for your organization.

post.description: Register a new webhook endpoint to receive event notifications. You can subscribe to specific events like message.received, conversation.created, etc.

delete.description: Permanently delete a webhook. This will stop all future event deliveries to this endpoint.

get.description: Retrieve details of a specific webhook including its configuration and health status.

patch.description: Update webhook configuration. You can change the URL, subscribed events, or enable/disable the webhook.

get.description: Retrieve recent delivery attempts for a webhook. Useful for debugging failed deliveries.

tags.description: View conversations and message history; open, close, assign, comment, and reply
tags.name: Conversations
